content: Foundation: adequate calcium (1000-1200mg/day total from diet + supplements) and vitamin D (800-2000 IU/day to maintain 25-OH vitamin D >30 ng/mL), weight-bearing exercise, fall prevention, smoking cessation. Pharmacologic therapy for T-score ≤-2.5, prior fragility fracture, or osteopenia with high FRAX risk. First-line: bisphosphonates (alendronate, risedronate PO weekly; zoledronic acid IV yearly) - inhibit osteoclasts, reduce vertebral/hip fractures. Alternatives: denosumab (RANKL inhibitor, SC q6months) - potent antiresorptive, reversible effect requires transition plan. Anabolic agents for severe osteoporosis (T-score <-3.0 or multiple fractures): teriparatide/abaloparatide (PTH analogs, daily SC, max 2 years); romosozumab (sclerostin inhibitor, monthly SC, 12 months) - increases formation, reduces resorption, cardiovascular risk warning. Drug holiday after 3-5 years bisphosphonates if fracture risk decreases. Monitor with repeat DEXA every 1-2 years.
